old_claim.note: Construction provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because the edge conflated general built space with Classical monumental construction.

source_shape.source_locator: What is the Library of Ashurbanipal section describes cuneiform tablets from Nineveh; the removed edge source was about Greek temples, not library operations.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source-backed library anchor is an Assyrian tablet collection; the previous edge source described Greek temple construction and did not support a library dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Library operation needs writing, storage, and administration, but the graph node construction is scoped to Classical monumental construction and is too late and too broad for this edge.

ontology_before: The library node treated Classical Monumental Construction as an enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: The library node remains connected to writing while avoiding a false dependency on later/scope-mismatched monumental construction.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Libraries must not directly depend on Classical Monumental Construction unless scoped to a built-library architecture node.
would_reject_if: A source shows this Libraries node is intentionally scoped only to purpose-built monumental library buildings.
A narrower built-library architecture node is introduced and this edge is re-scoped to that node.
The construction node is re-scoped and redated to generic storage/building construction that clearly predates the library anchor.
short_reason: The Greek-temple construction source did not support early Assyrian library operations.
